module1.clean_text:
Oran [DATE] Number: [ID] and first name: [PATIENT] [DATE] ORAN
Tel No: [ID] Report
This is the infant [PATIENT] aged [AGE] originally from and residing in ORAN, from a non-consanguineous middle-aged couple, the only child, hospitalized at our level for the investigation and PEC of respiratory distress with axial and segmental hypotonia. HISTORY:
Family:
Father: aged [AGE] in BES. Mother: aged [AGE] in BES, G1P1. Inbreeding: NO. No cases of death at young age in the family
No notion of neuromuscular diseases or disabilities in the family
Personal:
Physiological
Pregnancy: well monitored. MAF present in the last quarter
No concept of fetal distress
Full-term delivery, by vaginal route. Good Apgar at birth
Birth weight: 03Kg400. Breastfeeding: Mixed from birth. Vaccination: up to date. Pathological:
Axial and segmental hypotonia since birth
HISTORY OF THE DISEASE:
Going back a few days before his admission and as part of the investigation of his hypotonia, a consultation was made with a private pediatrician who noted moderate respiratory distress, hence his referral to our level and his admission for investigation and treatment. ADMISSION STATUS:
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 04kg800 [-0.59 DS]
Size:58cm [+0.45 DS]
PC: 40[ -0.3 DS]
General condition kept afebrile at 36.5
Hemodynamically stable with warm extremities, CRT<3 seconds. Normally colored connective integuments. Neurological examination:
FA: open at 02 cm, deep axial and segmental hypotonia,
good sucking reflex
Grasping reflex weak at 04 limbs, Moro reflex weak
ENT examination:
Nasal obstruction
No superficial lymphadenopathy
Respiratory examination:
FR: 40/min, SaO2: 98% under 03l/min of oxygen
Respiratory distress caused by: subcostal drawing with xiphoid funnel
pulmonary auscultation: fine crackles on auscultation. Cardiac exam:
HR: 135bpm, B1B2 clearly audible
No hiss, no added noise. Digestive examination:
Soft abdomen, preserved transit. No organomegaly
Urogenital examination:
male morphotype
no abnormalities of sexual differentiation
PARACLINICAL EXAMINATIONS:
BIOLOGY:
NFS: correct
CRP: Negative
Correct thyroid assessment
Normal muscle enzymes
No hepatic cytolysis
GAZOMETRY (11/24/2025): Slightly compensated grade II respiratory acidosis with hypercapnea at 55mmHg
RADIOLOGY:
Chest radiography: Multiple heterogeneous opacities in both lung fields
Echo-coeur: (05-11-2025) Small CIV without impact
Brain CT: (10-11-2025) Fine, harmonious atrophic appearance of the corpus callosum, no significant associated abnormalities
ENMG: (11/19/2025) Pure motor polyneuropathy predominantly proximal, due to axonal loss, of severe intensity which may be related to diffuse damage (staged and bilateral) of spinal motor neurons facing the anterior horns of the lumbosacrals from L3 to S1 and cervical from C5 to D1. A degenerative and progressive pathology of spinal motor neurons is strongly suspected (SMA suspected). In total:
This is the infant BELHOUARI ZAKARIA aged 02 months from a non-consanguineous couple, admitted to our service for the management of respiratory distress, who poses the following problems:
Respiratory distress
Profound peripheral hypotony
Mildly compensated respiratory acidosis with hypercapnea
Faced with this picture, a neuromuscular disease spinal muscular atrophy (SMA TYPE 1: Vernic Hoffman) is strongly suspected for which the genetic study in search of an SMN1, 2 mutation was launched confirming the diagnosis (homozygous state of a deletion of the SMN1 gene)
Therapeutic conduct:
Hospitalization with isolation measures.
Hygiene and dietetic measures
Placed on antibiotic therapy with good improvement
Placed on non-invasive ventilation
EVOLUTION DURING HOSPITALIZATION:
The evolution of the infant under NIV (PEEP: 05cmHg, AI: 05mmHg with an O2 flow rate of 0.5l/min) was marked by a clear improvement:
Regression of signs of struggle with good saturation 96-98% under 0.5l/min of O2
Respiratory rate increased from 73c/min to 38-40c/min
Heart rate increased from 200bpm to 120bpm
Control blood gas: PaCO2: 38mmHg
On D 06 of placing under NIV:
The child presented a worsening of his respiratory distress (reappearance of signs of struggle, FR: 62c/min with an increase in O2 requirements) of probably central origin; Currently, the infant is on permanent NIV (PEEP: 05cmHg, AI: 05cmHg with an O2 flow rate of 02l/min) with good saturation with cyanosis at times.
CONCLUSION:
This is the infant BELHOUARI ZAKARIA, aged 02 months, originally from and residing in ORAN, hospitalized at our level for the treatment of spinal muscular atrophy type 01 (homozygous state SMN 1 deletion)

Oran 11/23/2025
File Number: 16985
Name and first name: BELHOUARI ZAKARIA
Born: 09/24/2025
Address: ORAN
Tel No: 07.90.49.89.36
Medical Report
This is the infant BELHOUARI ZAKARIA, aged 02 months, originally from and residing in ORAN, from a non-consanguineous middle-aged couple, the only child, hospitalized at our level for the investigation and PEC of respiratory distress with axial and segmental hypotonia. HISTORY:
Family:
Father: aged 44, in BES. Mother: aged 35, in BES, G1P1. Inbreeding: NO. No cases of death at young age in the family
No notion of neuromuscular diseases or disabilities in the family
Personal:
Physiological
Pregnancy: well monitored. MAF present in the last quarter
No concept of fetal distress
Full-term delivery, by vaginal route. Good Apgar at birth
Birth weight: 03Kg400. Breastfeeding: Mixed from birth. Vaccination: up to date. Pathological:
Axial and segmental hypotonia since birth
HISTORY OF THE DISEASE:
Going back a few days before his admission and as part of the investigation of his hypotonia, a consultation was made with a private pediatrician who noted moderate respiratory distress, hence his referral to our level and his admission for investigation and treatment. ADMISSION STATUS:

Size:58cm [+0.45 DS]
PC: 40[ -0.3 DS]

good sucking reflex
Grasping reflex weak at 04 limbs, Moro reflex weak

pulmonary auscultation: fine crackles on auscultation. Cardiac exam:
HR: 135bpm, B1B2 clearly audible
No hiss, no added noise. Digestive examination:

Urogenital examination:
male morphotype
no abnormalities of sexual differentiation
PARACLINICAL EXAMINATIONS:
BIOLOGY:

CRP: Negative

RADIOLOGY:

Echo-coeur: (05-11-2025) Small CIV without impact
Brain CT: (10-11-2025) Fine, harmonious atrophic appearance of the corpus callosum, no significant associated abnormalities
ENMG: (11/19/2025) Pure motor polyneuropathy predominantly proximal, due to axonal loss, of severe intensity which may be related to diffuse damage (staged and bilateral) of spinal motor neurons facing the anterior horns of the lumbosacrals from L3 to S1 and cervical from C5 to D1. A degenerative and progressive pathology of spinal motor neurons is strongly suspected (SMA suspected). In total:
This is the infant BELHOUARI ZAKARIA aged 02 months from a non-consanguineous couple, admitted to our service for the management of respiratory distress, who poses the following problems:
Respiratory distress
Profound peripheral hypotony
Mildly compensated respiratory acidosis with hypercapnea
Faced with this picture, a neuromuscular disease spinal muscular atrophy (SMA TYPE 1: Vernic Hoffman) is strongly suspected for which the genetic study in search of an SMN1, 2 mutation was launched confirming the diagnosis (homozygous state of a deletion of the SMN1 gene)
Therapeutic conduct:

Hygiene and dietetic measures
Placed on antibiotic therapy with good improvement
Placed on non-invasive ventilation
EVOLUTION DURING HOSPITALIZATION:
The evolution of the infant under NIV (PEEP: 05cmHg, AI: 05mmHg with an O2 flow rate of 0.5l/min) was marked by a clear improvement:
Regression of signs of struggle with good saturation 96-98% under 0.5l/min of O2
Respiratory rate increased from 73c/min to 38-40c/min
Heart rate increased from 200bpm to 120bpm
Control blood gas: PaCO2: 38mmHg
On D 06 of placing under NIV:
The child presented a worsening of his respiratory distress (reappearance of signs of struggle, FR: 62c/min with an increase in O2 requirements) of probably central origin; Currently, the infant is on permanent NIV (PEEP: 05cmHg, AI: 05cmHg with an O2 flow rate of 02l/min) with good saturation with cyanosis at times.
